_comment: Machine-readable layer index for Truvio.Commerce.Distribution (P3, RUN-VERSION-CURRENCY T3-01..T3-04). SOURCE OF TRUTH for what layers exist, their kind/version/status, and what a retired name was superseded by. Two consumers read it: (1) tools/ci/Validate-Distribution.ps1 regenerates the `layers` array from the live tree and diffs it clean (drift both directions FAILs), asserts `gateProven` is present, and rejects any edition ref or living-doc mention of a name absent from `layers` (a retired hit names its successor); (2) the DemoAgent currency preflight (Invoke-DemoGate.ps1 `currency` leg) asserts this file parses + carries `gateProven`, and that every layer the demo consumed is live here (a retired hit -> 'retired -> use <supersededBy>'). UPDATE MECHANICS: the `layers` array is regenerated from the tree by Validate-Distribution.ps1 -RegenerateIndex (deterministic); `retired` tombstones are authored on layer retirement (release checklist, FOLLOWUP.md T4-02); `gateProven` is stamped by the FOUNDRY publish flow at release time (tools/harness/Write-IndexGateProven.ps1) from the gate run(s) that proved this main -- never hand-authored as trusted. Consumers 'pin origin/main + assert INDEX.gateProven present' (D-CONSUME a).

gateProven.note: Latest gate-proven main (RUN-DISTRIBUTION-QUALITY P5 sweep, DW 10.28.1-PreRelease). All four editions are gate-proven on this main. dap-portal's earlier Step 10d3 Area style-wiring defect (run 20260717-033113 FAIL) was fixed in surface-dap-portal 1.0.3 (published in PR #15) and re-gated GREEN at run 20260717-034401 -- the latest dap-portal verdict. An edition may be temporarily absent from gateProven without invalidating the marker.

layers.name: feature-reordering-pricing
layers.note: TOMBSTONED (RUN-DISTRIBUTION-QUALITY D-D). Over-bundled layer split into feature-reordering@1.0.0 + feature-pricing@1.0.0. No edition composes it; retained one release for consumers still pinning 1.2.1, then removed. Do NOT add to new editions.

retired.name: theme-nav-polish
retired.note: Retired nav-polish theme overlay (the RUN-VERSION-CURRENCY incident layer). Hard-deleted with no tombstone in P0; tombstoned here (L-04). Layer tag deleted P1 T1-03. Successor: the shipped default theme.

retired.name: overlay-nav-polish
retired.note: Rename of theme-nav-polish (the 2.4.3/2.4.4 tree name). Same retirement; layer tag deleted P1 T1-03. Successor: the shipped default theme.

retired.name: theme-fashion-lifestyle
retired.note: One of the 3 demo themes retired when the overlay concept was dropped and presentation consolidated into a single default theme (theme-default 1.0.0).

retired.name: theme-industrial-b2b
retired.note: One of the 3 demo themes retired into theme-default 1.0.0 (overlay concept dropped).

retired.name: theme-tech-saas
retired.note: One of the 3 demo themes retired into theme-default 1.0.0 (overlay concept dropped).

retired.name: bom-configurator
retired.note: Pre-rename name; renamed to feature-bom-configurator when layer dir names took their kind prefix.

retired.name: subscription-orders
retired.note: Pre-rename name; renamed to feature-subscription-orders (kind prefix).

retired.name: reordering-pricing
retired.note: Pre-rename name; became feature-reordering-pricing (kind prefix), then split into feature-reordering + feature-pricing. Points at the current live successors, not the deprecated bundle.

retired.name: dap-portal
retired.note: Pre-rename LAYER name; renamed to surface-dap-portal (kind prefix). NB: an EDITION named dap-portal (editions/dap-portal.json) is live and unrelated -- this tombstone is the retired layer only.

retired.name: headless
retired.note: Pre-rename LAYER name; renamed to surface-headless (kind prefix). NB: an EDITION named headless-demo is live and unrelated.

retired.name: catalog-fixture
retired.note: Absorbed into sample-data 2.0.0 (all content shipped as SQL layer files). See commit c066e52.

retired.name: fixture-catalog
retired.note: Earliest name; renamed to catalog-fixture, then absorbed into sample-data 2.0.0.
